issues.evidence: Initial draft had a fog-side voice say, "你们车里有系统是不是？" The revised draft changes this to suspicion of the visible central screen: "你们车里那块屏亮了是不是？"
issues.suggested_fix: Resolved in revised draft. Keep outsiders able to suspect the RV is special without knowing the golden-finger term.

issues.evidence: The draft makes grey-white vine residue a candidate for Simple Medical Bay isolation /判定, but it does not acquire the complete material set or activate the module.
issues.suggested_fix: In revision and state updates, keep the residue as an unisolated, unjudged candidate. Do not mark Simple Medical Bay as unlocked or usable.

issues.evidence: The draft stays at Qinghe medical-point exterior. It uses the left wall, lamp line, No. 2 power box signal, and exterior pressure without entering the main building or explaining the survivor organization.
issues.suggested_fix: Preserve exterior-only scope. Chapter 14 can move into material contest without revealing the whole Qinghe order.

issues.evidence: Lu Chenzhou keeps the RV running, preserves the retreat angle, refuses to open the door, abandons contaminated tools, and uses lights / reverse / external speaker / rope / rod rather than body contact.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep these as the core chapter payoff. Do not revise into a door-opening rescue or direct melee.

issues.evidence: The child and abdominal bleeding man remain outside as controllable / observable subjects; Zhou Xiaohe stays in temporary observation and only contributes a sound cue.
issues.suggested_fix: State updates should not upgrade any of them to crew, boarding status, or formal allies.

issues.evidence: The draft shows the vine reacting to blood, supplies, sound response, contact, and door direction, while leaving the underlying ecology unexplained.
issues.suggested_fix: Record these as observed reactions only. Avoid turning them into complete rules or taxonomy.

proposed_state_updates: Chapter 13 at Qinghe medical-point exterior: the grey-eyed patient mutates into a grey-white vine threat that can exploit medical supplies, blood traces, sound response, contact attempts, and the RV door direction.

Lu Chenzhou keeps the RV running and angled for retreat, refuses to open the door, and completes the first non-contact vine counter using lights, reverse movement, external speaker commands, rope / rod tools, and vehicle angle.

Lin Zhixia observes the vine's reactions without touching it, identifies the thrown medical bag as contaminated, stops wrong contact, and treats the observed triggers as provisional rather than final ecology.
The abdominal bleeding man and low-fever child remain outside and survive this beat only by obeying left-wall / no-contact instructions; neither gains boarding status.
A wrong-contact survivor who tries to grab the contaminated supplies is nearly wrapped, demonstrating that attempts to recover medical supplies or touch the vine can accelerate danger.
The thrown medical bag, rope / rod contact tools, and exterior supplies involved in the countermeasure are contaminated or abandoned rather than recovered into the RV.

Grey-white vine fragments / residue remain near the water crack as a possible Simple Medical Bay isolation-material clue, but they are unisolated, unjudged, and forbidden from entering the living area.

The Simple Medical Bay remains locked and inactive; the chapter ends with the material signal behind the residue and a short remaining filter window pushing chapter 14 toward a controlled material contest.
human_approval_needed: Approve grey-white vine as Qinghe's first physical vine-mutated patient threat.
Approve the observed vine reactions to blood, medical supplies, sound response, contact attempts, and door direction as provisional behavior, not complete ecology.
Approve contaminated vine residue as a possible Simple Medical Bay material clue only after isolation and system判定.
Approve tool / supply loss from the non-contact countermeasure.
Approve that no outside survivor currently knows the RV 'system' by name; any such wording must be removed before acceptance.
